verdict.answer_first: A starts with the core definition immediately: "each fact lives in exactly one place"; B first gives a section title, then a similar definition.
verdict.decisiveness: A gives one clearer prescription: "normalize to 3NF first, then denormalize only where a real, measured performance problem justifies it"; B adds several guidance bullets and broad cases.
verdict.fabrication: B uses more unverified illustrative numbers/claims: "joins 8 tables", "a million comments", "catastrophically slow"; A also has "4,000 order rows" but less often.
verdict.actionability: A is easier to save as a rule: default 3NF, measure, denormalize only for proven read bottlenecks or snapshots; B is useful but more scattered.
